pages.rationale: This is the cover page of the document and contains no factual content for question generation.
This page contains the table of contents and provides no conceptual or factual depth for questions.
Continuation of the table of contents; contains no testable material.
Introductory note explaining the document structure and features; no prelims-relevant content.
Contains fundamental definitions (Gene, DNA, Genome) and details on India's first gene-edited sheep using CRISPR-Cas9.

pages.subtopic: Gene Editing Techniques and Sickle Cell Disease
pages.rationale: Covers types of gene editing (Somatic vs Germline), specific techniques (ZFNs, TALENs, CRISPR), and the BIRSA-101 therapy for Sickle Cell Disease.
High-density page covering GM crop status in India, the regulatory framework (GEAC, FSSAI), and Site Directed Nuclease (SDN) classifications.

pages.subtopic: Genome India Project and Gene-Drive Technology
pages.rationale: Discusses genome-edited rice varieties, the Genome India Project, and the mechanics of Gene-Drive Technology.
Explains Organ-on-chip technology, RNA silencing mechanisms (siRNA, miRNA), and the concept of a Pangenome.
Covers the SHUKR gene, DNA profiling methods (STR, mtDNA, SNP), Jumping Genes, and the launch of NexCAR19.

pages.subtopic: CAR-T Therapy and Mitochondrial Replacement Therapy
pages.rationale: Detailed explanation of CAR-T therapy mechanics and the principles of Mitochondrial Replacement Therapy (MRT).

pages.subtopic: MRT Techniques and Stem Cell Types
pages.rationale: Dense conceptual content on Three-Parent Babies (PNT vs MST) and detailed classification of stem cells (ESCs, iPSCs, ASCs).
Highly relevant content on the Phenome India Project, BioE3 Policy, and the National Biofoundry Network.
Contains information on the DEEP project and epigenetics, but a significant portion of the page is an advertisement.
Very dense page defining AI stages (ANI, AGI, ASI), ML types, NLP, Neural Networks, and Agentic AI.
Covers the Bharat Gen initiative, IndiaAI Mission pillars, and global AI regulatory frameworks like the Bletchley Declaration.

pages.subtopic: Deepfakes and AI in Biological Research
pages.rationale: Explains deepfake technology (GANs) and various AI models in biology like AlphaFold and AlphaGenome.
Briefly mentions TPUs and economic paradoxes (Jevons, Solow), but the majority of the page is an advertisement.
This page provides foundational definitions for quantum mechanics, qubits, superposition, entanglement, and interference, which are high-yield conceptual topics for UPSC Prelims.

pages.subtopic: Nobel Prize in Physics 2025 and Quantum Tunnelling
pages.rationale: Covers the 2025 Nobel Prize in Physics, explaining macroscopic quantum tunnelling and Josephson Junctions, which are highly relevant current affairs topics.
Contains detailed objectives of the National Quantum Mission and the benefits of Quantum Key Distribution (QKD), including India's first extensive QKD network.
Explains the mechanism and applications of BCIs and the concept of Quantum Information Scrambling, both emerging technology areas for the exam.
Discusses the advantages and applications of 3D and 4D printing, along with Neuralink's Blindsight chip, providing good factual content for technology-based questions.

pages.subtopic: Stablecoins and Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC)
pages.rationale: Provides a comparison between stablecoins and CBDCs, including the Indian regulatory framework for virtual digital assets (VDAs).

pages.subtopic: Bluetooth Mesh, Undersea Cables, and Vikram3201 Processor
pages.rationale: Covers diverse topics including Bluetooth mesh networking, the Waterworth undersea cable project, and India's indigenous 32-bit processor.

pages.subtopic: RFID (FASTag), Digital Twin, and Robotic Surgery
pages.rationale: Explains the technology behind FASTag (RFID), the concept of Digital Twins, and India's indigenous surgical robot SSI Mantra.
Detailed explanation of black hole mergers, stellar evolution stages, and the properties of gravitational waves, which are classic UPSC science topics.
Focuses on neutrino properties, the JUNO observatory in China, and the India-based Neutrino Observatory (INO).

pages.subtopic: CERN, Higgs Boson, and Matter-Antimatter Asymmetry
pages.rationale: High-density content on CERN's Large Hadron Collider, the Higgs Boson, and the fundamental physics problem of matter-antimatter asymmetry.

pages.subtopic: Ordinary Matter vs Dark Matter and FRBs
pages.rationale: Compares ordinary and dark matter and explains Fast Radio Bursts (FRBs). Quota is slightly lower due to a large advertisement on the page.
Provides technical details and objectives of the NISAR mission, a critical bilateral space project between India and the USA.
Dense page covering India's human spaceflight mission (Gaganyaan), the Bharatiya Antariksh Station, and the Aditya-L1 solar mission including Lagrange points.
Covers satellite docking technology (SpaDeX), Chandrayaan-3's thermal probe findings, and NASA's GRAIL mission for lunar gravity mapping.

pages.subtopic: Axiom-4 Mission and International Space Station
pages.rationale: Details the Axiom-4 private mission involving an Indian astronaut and provides facts about the International Space Station (ISS).

pages.subtopic: Commercial EO Satellites and Polar Orbits
pages.rationale: Discusses the rise of private sector participation in Earth Observation (EO) satellites and the characteristics of Earth's polar orbits.

pages.subtopic: Satellite Orbits and Key ISRO Missions
pages.rationale: Contains a highly useful comparison table of different satellite orbits (LEO, SSO, MEO, GEO) and summaries of key missions like Astrosat, VOM, and XPoSat.

This page contains a high-density table of international space missions (Lucy, Tianwen-2, Biomass, etc.) and technical details on India's CE20 cryogenic engine, providing multiple factual and conceptual question opportunities.
Detailed information on NavIC (IRNSS) configuration, coverage, and strategic advantages, plus a global comparison of navigation systems and the HOPE analog mission in Ladakh.
Dense comparison of ISRO's launch vehicle fleet (PSLV, GSLV, LVM3, SSLV) and details on the upcoming Lunar Module Launch Vehicle (LMLV).
Covers satellite internet infrastructure and major global projects, alongside the classification and characteristics of solar flares and CMEs.
Explains the solar activity cycle, space debris (Kessler Syndrome), and international/national initiatives like NETRA and the Zero Debris Charter.
High conceptual density regarding Brown Dwarfs, Super Earths, Extreme Nuclear Transients, and technical details of the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST).
Details on the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS, the Kodaikanal Solar Observatory (Evershed Effect), and the private Vikram-1 launch vehicle.
Provides detailed classifications of TB (Pulmonary, MDR, XDR) and HIV treatment/prevention (Lenacapavir, PrEP, ART), which are high-priority UPSC topics.
Covers India's elimination of Trachoma, Hepatitis classifications (B, C, D), Mpox characteristics, and HPV screening developments.
Explains the 2025 Nobel Prize in Medicine regarding peripheral immune tolerance, Treg cells, and the FOXP3 gene, offering deep conceptual question potential.
Discusses Antimicrobial Resistance drivers and the regulatory framework for Fixed-Dose Combination (FDC) drugs in India.
Provides a comprehensive typology of vaccines (Live, Inactivated, Subunit, etc.) and details on the indigenous AdFalciVax malaria vaccine.
Explains oncolytic, personalized mRNA, and self-amplifying mRNA (saRNA) vaccines, along with the WHO Pandemic Agreement.
Details the Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) disease list, Monoclonal Antibodies (mAbs), and the National One Health Mission.
Covers transfat regulations, the mechanism of GLP-1 based weight loss drugs (Wegovy), and FSSAI directives on ORS.
Discusses the newly recognized Type 5 diabetes and SPARC radiation therapy. The quota is lower due to a large advertisement block at the bottom.
Highly factual page detailing S-400, Barak 8, Akash, and the Akashteer automated control system.
Dense information on IADWS, Project Kusha, and a comprehensive comparison of global air defence systems like Iron Dome, THAAD, and Patriot.

This page provides a detailed comparison between cruise and ballistic missiles across multiple parameters like path, range, and payload, which is a high-yield area for UPSC. It also introduces Agni-5.
Contains critical details on Agni-5, the Strategic Forces Command, and the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP), including specific missile types (Prithvi, Trishul, etc.).
Provides a comprehensive table of various missiles (BrahMos, Astra, Pralay, etc.) and starts the technical explanation of Scramjet engines.
Explains the technical differences between Ramjet and Scramjet engines, hypersonic weapon types (HGV, HCM), and the GSAT-7R satellite.

pages.subtopic: Military Satellites and Directed Energy Weapons
pages.rationale: Covers the GSAT-7 series for different wings of the armed forces and introduces Directed Energy Weapons (DEW) like the Mk-II (A) LASER system.
Discusses Stratospheric Airship Platforms (HAPS) and the evolution of fighter jet generations (4th to 5th), including the AMCA programme.
Details specific platforms like Apache, LCA Mark-1A, Rafale Marine, and naval ships like INS Arnala and INS Nistar, plus the Rudrastra UAV.

pages.subtopic: UAVs, Glide Bombs, and Naval Projects
pages.rationale: Covers Bhargavastra anti-drone system, Gaurav glide bomb, Fiber Optic drones, and Project 17A stealth frigates.
A thin page focusing only on the Netra MkII airborne early warning and control system.
Extremely high-value comparison between fission and fusion, plus details on the ITER project and India's contribution to it.
Details Indian Tokamak research (ADITYA) and the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR), including the significance of the three-stage program.
Explains India's three-stage nuclear programme and the technical process of Uranium enrichment.
Covers Thorium Molten Salt Reactors, India's thorium reserves, and the basics of Li-ion battery technology including 'Black Mass'.
Compares Sodium-Ion, Vanadium Redox Flow, and Solid-State batteries, highlighting their advantages over conventional Li-ion.
Explains GIS, GPS, and Remote Sensing, alongside Operation Dronagiri and the National Geospatial Policy 2022.
Lists the 30 critical minerals identified by India and discusses international collaborations like the Minerals Security Partnership.
Provides data on global REE reserves, their distribution in India, and an introduction to nanotechnology concepts.
Covers specific nano-applications (Nanozymes, Nano-fertilizers) and the 2025 Nobel Prize-winning Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOF).

This page contains dense technical information on MOFs (features and applications) and the India Electric Mobility Index (IEMI), including EV types and the PM E-DRIVE scheme, which are highly relevant for UPSC Prelims.

pages.subtopic: V2G Technology, Hyperloop, and Deep Ocean Mission
pages.rationale: Covers Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology, the Hyperloop mobility system (LIM-based), and the initial details of the Samudrayaan Mission, providing multiple factual points for question generation.

pages.subtopic: Deep Ocean Mission and RDI Scheme
pages.rationale: Detailed information on the Matsya-6000 submersible, components of the Deep Ocean Mission (DOM), and the Research Development and Innovation (RDI) Scheme including the Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF).

pages.subtopic: SODAR, Kavach, Shinkansen, and Aviation Tech
pages.rationale: Extremely dense page covering five distinct technologies: SODAR (meteorology), HANSA-3 (aviation), Kavach (rail safety), Shinkansen (high-speed rail), and Black Box (flight data). Each section provides specific technical facts.
Provides conceptual clarity on Altermagnets (CrSb), the working principle and applications of Accelerometers, and a comparison between Thermal and Membrane desalination technologies.
Covers Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS), the regulatory framework for Biostimulants, the Agrienics Programme, and the historical/institutional context of the Metre Convention.
Promotional material for coaching services and past results; no academic content for question generation.
